data.title: Assessing UFP Technologies (UFPT) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
data.description:
UFP Technologies (UFPT) has drawn fresh attention after its recent share price move, with the stock closing at US$258.68. Investors are weighing that level against the company’s longer term return profile.

See our latest analysis for UFP Technologies.

That soft 1 day share price return of 2.99% and 7 day share price return of 0.71% comes after a stronger run, with a 30 day share price return of 9.05% and a 90 day share price return of 29.33%. The 3 year total shareholder return of about 2.4x and 5 year total shareholder return of about 5.6x point to a long running, if sometimes volatile, story where recent momentum is still building.

If UFP Technologies has caught your eye, this could be a good moment to broaden your watchlist and check out other healthcare stocks that might fit a similar thesis.

So with the shares sitting around US$258.68, a value score of 1, analyst targets closer to US$329.50, and recent total returns mixed across timeframes, the key question is whether there is still a buying opportunity here or whether the market is already pricing in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 21.5% Undervalued

With UFP Technologies last closing at $258.68 against a narrative fair value of $329.50, the current setup centers on whether earnings and margins can evolve to support that higher price over time.

The expansion of manufacturing capacity and product development centers in the Dominican Republic, along with new program launches for robotic-assisted surgery customers, sets the stage for sustained revenue growth tied to increased demand for sophisticated medical device components and packaging solutions.

Want to see what is baked into that higher value? Revenue trends, margin lift and a richer earnings multiple all sit at the core of this narrative. Curious which assumptions really move the needle on that target number?

The narrative fair value of $329.50 paints UFP Technologies as undervalued, but the current P/E of 29.7x tells a tighter story. It sits below the US Medical Equipment industry average of 32x, yet well above a fair ratio of 20.6x and a peer average of 23.4x. This points to a valuation that already appears to incorporate a significant degree of optimism. The question is whether you think the business can keep justifying that premium.

data.title: Weaker Earnings And New Director Appointment Could Be A Game Changer For Westamerica Bancorporation (WABC)
data.description:
• Westamerica Bancorporation recently reported fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results showing year-over-year declines in net interest income, net income, and earnings per share, while also declaring a quarterly cash dividend of US$0.46 per share payable on February 13, 2026.
  • In addition, the board appointed marketing and business strategy veteran Carter Welch as an independent director effective February 26, 2026, signaling a potential shift in how the bank thinks about growth and client engagement.
  • We’ll now examine how the weaker earnings, particularly lower net interest income, shape Westamerica Bancorporation’s investment narrative for investors.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Westamerica Bancorporation's Investment Narrative?

To own Westamerica Bancorporation today, you really have to believe in a conservative regional bank that can manage through pressure on net interest income while still rewarding shareholders with consistent dividends. The latest results confirm that earnings are moving in the wrong direction, with both full-year and fourth-quarter profit and net interest income lower than a year ago, so the near-term story hinges on whether that pressure stabilizes or worsens. The reaffirmed US$0.46 quarterly dividend suggests management is comfortable with the balance sheet for now, but it also raises the question of how much flexibility the bank has if earnings continue to soften. The appointment of marketing and business strategy veteran Carter Welch does not immediately shift the earnings outlook, yet it hints that the board is at least open to fresh thinking around growth and client engagement, which could influence future catalysts. For the moment, though, the more immediate risks still sit squarely with earnings compression and relatively low forecast returns.

However, one risk that stands out here is how persistent earnings pressure could constrain options.
Our expertly prepared valuation report on Westamerica Bancorporation implies its share price may be lower than expected.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s single fair value view at US$42 contrasts with current pricing, while recent earnings declines and low forecast returns underline why many investors may be cautious and looking for multiple viewpoints.

data.title: Doxee (BIT:DOX) swells 11% this week, taking one-year gains to 135%
data.description:
The most you can lose on any stock (assuming you don't use leverage) is 100% of your money. But if you pick the right business to buy shares in, you can make more than you can lose. For example, the Doxee S.p.A. (BIT:DOX) share price has soared 135% return in just a single year. It's even up 11% in the last week. On the other hand, longer term shareholders have had a tougher run, with the stock falling 57% in three years.

On the back of a solid 7-day performance, let's check what role the company's fundamentals have played in driving long term shareholder returns.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Given that Doxee didn't make a profit in the last twelve months, we'll focus on revenue growth to form a quick view of its business development. Generally speaking, companies without profits are expected to grow revenue every year, and at a good clip. As you can imagine, fast revenue growth, when maintained, often leads to fast profit growth.

Over the last twelve months, Doxee's revenue grew by 9.1%. That's not a very high growth rate considering it doesn't make profits. In contrast, the share price took off during the year, gaining 135%. The business will need a lot more growth to justify that increase. We're not so sure that revenue growth is driving the market optimism about the stock.

The graphic below depicts how earnings and revenue have changed over time (unveil the exact values by clicking on the image).

You can see how its balance sheet has strengthened (or weakened) over time in this free interactive graphic.

Advertisement

A Different Perspective

We're pleased to report that Doxee shareholders have received a total shareholder return of 135% over one year. That gain is better than the annual TSR over five years, which is 2%. Therefore it seems like sentiment around the company has been positive lately. In the best case scenario, this may hint at some real business momentum, implying that now could be a great time to delve deeper. It's always interesting to track share price performance over the longer term. But to understand Doxee better, we need to consider many other factors. For example, we've discovered 2 warning signs for Doxee (1 can't be ignored!) that you should be aware of before investing here.

But note: Doxee may not be the best stock to buy. So take a peek at this free list of interesting companies with past earnings growth (and further growth forecast).

Please note, the market returns quoted in this article reflect the market weighted average returns of stocks that currently trade on Italian exchanges.

data.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Valuation Check After Breakthrough Therapy Status For Sofetabart Mipitecan
data.description:
The FDA’s Breakthrough Therapy designation for Eli Lilly (LLY) candidate sofetabart mipitecan in platinum resistant ovarian cancer has pushed oncology back into focus for a stock many investors mainly associate with obesity drugs.

See our latest analysis for Eli Lilly.

The Breakthrough Therapy news lands after a busy start to the year for Eli Lilly, with an AI co-innovation lab launched alongside NVIDIA, new distribution for its obesity medicines through CenterWell Pharmacy, and fresh legal scrutiny around GLP 1 compounding. While the 1 day share price return of 2.12% and 30 day share price return of 1.25% show some near term volatility, a 90 day share price return of 28.78% and a 1 year total shareholder return of 36.52% point to momentum that has been building on top of very large 3 year and 5 year total shareholder returns.

If you are looking beyond Eli Lilly and want more ideas in the same space, this could be a good moment to scan healthcare stocks for other healthcare names on your radar.

With Eli Lilly now trading at $1,064.29, sitting only about 4% below the average analyst price target and implying roughly a 14% intrinsic discount, you have to ask yourself: is this still a buying opportunity, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 10.5% Undervalued

According to the most followed narrative on Eli Lilly, the fair value of $1,189.18 sits comfortably above the last close of $1,064.29, framing the current debate around how much of the GLP 1 story is already in the price.

Mounjaro/Zepbound: Lilly’s tirzepatide franchise is the engine of growth. Mounjaro (for type 2 diabetes) and Zepbound (obesity) each grew rapidly in 2024. Analysts project Mounjaro sales of $18.4 B in 2025 and $22.8B in 2026, and Zepbound jumping from $4.9B (2024) to $12.5 B in 2025 (and $18.1B in 2026). In other words, Lilly’s tirzepatide sales are expected to surpass Novo’s by 2026.

Read the complete narrative.

Want to see what sits behind that $1,189.18 fair value call? According to eat_dis_watermelon, it hinges on fast compounding revenue, rising profitability, and a rich future earnings multiple that looks more like a high growth tech profile than a traditional pharma giant.

Result: Fair Value of $1,189.18 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, this hinges on premium GLP 1 pricing and smooth capacity expansion, and any pricing pressure, safety scare, or facility delay could quickly weaken that thesis.

Find out about the key risks to this Eli Lilly narrative.

Another View: What The P/E Ratio Is Saying

Our DCF work suggests Eli Lilly at $1,064.29 trades about 13.7% below an estimated fair value of $1,233.91, which points to an undervalued setup. Yet the market is already paying 51.8x earnings versus 19.8x for the US pharma industry, 16.8x for peers, and a fair ratio of 42.2x. This raises the question of whether this reflects a quality premium or a valuation risk that is building up.

data.title: Inari Amertron Berhad (KLSE:INARI) Stock's On A Decline: Are Poor Fundamentals The Cause?
data.description:
With its stock down 31% over the past three months, it is easy to disregard Inari Amertron Berhad (KLSE:INARI). Given that stock prices are usually driven by a company’s fundamentals over the long term, which in this case look pretty weak, we decided to study the company's key financial indicators. In this article, we decided to focus on Inari Amertron Berhad's ROE.

Return on Equity or ROE is a test of how effectively a company is growing its value and managing investors’ money. In other words, it is a profitability ratio which measures the rate of return on the capital provided by the company's shareholders.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

The formula for return on equity is:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Inari Amertron Berhad is:

7.8% = RM241m ÷ RM3.1b (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the amount earned after tax over the last twelve months. So, this means that for every MYR1 of its shareholder's investments, the company generates a profit of MYR0.08.

View our latest analysis for Inari Amertron Berhad

What Is The Relationship Between ROE And Earnings Growth?

So far, we've learned that ROE is a measure of a company's profitability. Based on how much of its profits the company chooses to reinvest or "retain", we are then able to evaluate a company's future ability to generate profits. Generally speaking, other things being equal, firms with a high return on equity and profit retention, have a higher growth rate than firms that don’t share these attributes.

Inari Amertron Berhad's Earnings Growth And 7.8% ROE

When you first look at it, Inari Amertron Berhad's ROE doesn't look that attractive. However, its ROE is similar to the industry average of 8.1%, so we won't completely dismiss the company. But Inari Amertron Berhad saw a five year net income decline of 4.3% over the past five years. Bear in mind, the company does have a slightly low ROE. Hence, this goes some way in explaining the shrinking earnings.

Next, we compared Inari Amertron Berhad's performance against the industry and found that the industry shrunk its earnings at 5.6% in the same period, which suggests that the company's earnings have been shrinking at a slower rate than its industry, This does offer shareholders some relief

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. What investors need to determine next is if the expected earnings growth, or the lack of it, is already built into the share price. By doing so, they will have an idea if the stock is headed into clear blue waters or if swampy waters await. If you're wondering about Inari Amertron Berhad's's valuation, check out this gauge of its price-to-earnings ratio, as compared to its industry.

Is Inari Amertron Berhad Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

Inari Amertron Berhad's declining earnings is not surprising given how the company is spending most of its profits in paying dividends, judging by its three-year median payout ratio of 96% (or a retention ratio of 3.9%). The business is only left with a small pool of capital to reinvest - A vicious cycle that doesn't benefit the company in the long-run.

Additionally, Inari Amertron Berhad has paid dividends over a period of at least ten years, which means that the company's management is determined to pay dividends even if it means little to no earnings growth. Based on the latest analysts' estimates, we found that the company's future payout ratio over the next three years is expected to hold steady at 86%. However, Inari Amertron Berhad's ROE is predicted to rise to 12% despite there being no anticipated change in its payout ratio.

Conclusion

In total, we would have a hard think before deciding on any investment action concerning Inari Amertron Berhad. Particularly, its ROE is a huge disappointment, not to mention its lack of proper reinvestment into the business. As a result its earnings growth has also been quite disappointing. That being so, the latest industry analyst forecasts show that the analysts are expecting to see a huge improvement in the company's earnings growth rate. To know more about the latest analysts predictions for the company, check out this visualization of analyst forecasts for the company.

data.title: Avenue Supermarts (NSE:DMART) Is Experiencing Growth In Returns On Capital
data.description:
There are a few key trends to look for if we want to identify the next multi-bagger. Typically, we'll want to notice a trend of growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and alongside that, an expanding base of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. So when we looked at Avenue Supermarts (NSE:DMART) and its trend of ROCE, we really liked what we saw.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

For those who don't know, ROCE is a measure of a company's yearly pre-tax profit (its return), relative to the capital employed in the business. To calculate this metric for Avenue Supermarts, this is the formula:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.16 = ₹39b ÷ (₹269b - ₹28b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to December 2025).

Thus, Avenue Supermarts has an ROCE of 16%. In absolute terms, that's a satisfactory return, but compared to the Consumer Retailing industry average of 10% it's much better.

Check out our latest analysis for Avenue Supermarts

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Avenue Supermarts compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Avenue Supermarts for free.

What Does the ROCE Trend For Avenue Supermarts Tell Us?

Investors would be pleased with what's happening at Avenue Supermarts. The data shows that returns on capital have increased substantially over the last five years to 16%. Basically the business is earning more per dollar of capital invested and in addition to that, 108% more capital is being employed now too. This can indicate that there's plenty of opportunities to invest capital internally and at ever higher rates, a combination that's common among multi-baggers.

In Conclusion...

In summary, it's great to see that Avenue Supermarts can compound returns by consistently reinvesting capital at increasing rates of return, because these are some of the key ingredients of those highly sought after multi-baggers. Since the stock has only returned 38% to shareholders over the last five years, the promising fundamentals may not be recognized yet by investors. So exploring more about this stock could uncover a good opportunity, if the valuation and other metrics stack up.

Before jumping to any conclusions though, we need to know what value we're getting for the current share price. That's where you can check out our FREE intrinsic value estimation for DMART that compares the share price and estimated value.

While Avenue Supermarts may not currently earn the highest returns, we've compiled a list of companies that currently earn more than 25% return on equity. Check out this free list here.

data.title: Is Scanfil Oyj (HEL:SCANFL) Using Too Much Debt?
data.description:
Howard Marks put it nicely when he said that, rather than worrying about share price volatility, 'The possibility of permanent loss is the risk I worry about... and every practical investor I know worries about.' So it might be obvious that you need to consider debt, when you think about how risky any given stock is, because too much debt can sink a company. We can see that Scanfil Oyj (HEL:SCANFL) does use debt in its business. But the real question is whether this debt is making the company risky.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

When Is Debt Dangerous?

Debt is a tool to help businesses grow, but if a business is incapable of paying off its lenders, then it exists at their mercy. In the worst case scenario, a company can go bankrupt if it cannot pay its creditors. However, a more common (but still painful) scenario is that it has to raise new equity capital at a low price, thus permanently diluting shareholders. Of course, plenty of companies use debt to fund growth, without any negative consequences. When we think about a company's use of debt, we first look at cash and debt together.

What Is Scanfil Oyj's Net Debt?

The image below, which you can click on for greater detail, shows that Scanfil Oyj had debt of €38.4m at the end of September 2025, a reduction from €41.9m over a year. But it also has €56.0m in cash to offset that, meaning it has €17.6m net cash.

How Healthy Is Scanfil Oyj's Balance Sheet?

We can see from the most recent balance sheet that Scanfil Oyj had liabilities of €207.6m falling due within a year, and liabilities of €46.1m due beyond that. On the other hand, it had cash of €56.0m and €180.5m worth of receivables due within a year. So its liabilities outweigh the sum of its cash and (near-term) receivables by €17.2m.

Since publicly traded Scanfil Oyj shares are worth a total of €694.9m, it seems unlikely that this level of liabilities would be a major threat. But there are sufficient liabilities that we would certainly recommend shareholders continue to monitor the balance sheet, going forward. While it does have liabilities worth noting, Scanfil Oyj also has more cash than debt, so we're pretty confident it can manage its debt safely.

View our latest analysis for Scanfil Oyj

Scanfil Oyj's EBIT was pretty flat over the last year, but that shouldn't be an issue given the it doesn't have a lot of debt. There's no doubt that we learn most about debt from the balance sheet. But ultimately the future profitability of the business will decide if Scanfil Oyj can strengthen its balance sheet over time. So if you want to see what the professionals think, you might find this free report on analyst profit forecasts to be interesting.

Finally, a business needs free cash flow to pay off debt; accounting profits just don't cut it. While Scanfil Oyj has net cash on its balance sheet, it's still worth taking a look at its ability to convert earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) to free cash flow, to help us understand how quickly it is building (or eroding) that cash balance. Happily for any shareholders, Scanfil Oyj actually produced more free cash flow than EBIT over the last three years. That sort of strong cash generation warms our hearts like a puppy in a bumblebee suit.

Summing Up

We could understand if investors are concerned about Scanfil Oyj's liabilities, but we can be reassured by the fact it has has net cash of €17.6m. And it impressed us with free cash flow of €51m, being 100% of its EBIT. So is Scanfil Oyj's debt a risk? It doesn't seem so to us. Over time, share prices tend to follow earnings per share, so if you're interested in Scanfil Oyj, you may well want to click here to check an interactive graph of its earnings per share history.

If, after all that, you're more interested in a fast growing company with a rock-solid balance sheet, then check out our list of net cash growth stocks without delay.

data.title: While institutions invested in Hankook Tire & Technology Co., Ltd. (KRX:161390) benefited from last week's 8.4% gain, public companies stood to gain the most
data.description:
Advertisement

Key Insights

  • The considerable ownership by public companies in Hankook Tire & Technology indicates that they collectively have a greater say in management and business strategy
  • The top 4 shareholders own 53% of the company
  • Insiders own 13% of Hankook Tire & Technology

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Every investor in Hankook Tire & Technology Co., Ltd. (KRX:161390) should be aware of the most powerful shareholder groups. We can see that public companies own the lion's share in the company with 31% ownership. That is, the group stands to benefit the most if the stock rises (or lose the most if there is a downturn).

While public companies were the group that benefitted the most from last week’s ₩634b market cap gain, institutions too had a 30% share in those profits.

In the chart below, we zoom in on the different ownership groups of Hankook Tire & Technology.

View our latest analysis for Hankook Tire & Technology

What Does The Institutional Ownership Tell Us About Hankook Tire & Technology?

Institutional investors commonly compare their own returns to the returns of a commonly followed index. So they generally do consider buying larger companies that are included in the relevant benchmark index.

As you can see, institutional investors have a fair amount of stake in Hankook Tire & Technology. This suggests some credibility amongst professional investors. But we can't rely on that fact alone since institutions make bad investments sometimes, just like everyone does. When multiple institutions own a stock, there's always a risk that they are in a 'crowded trade'. When such a trade goes wrong, multiple parties may compete to sell stock fast. This risk is higher in a company without a history of growth. You can see Hankook Tire & Technology's historic earnings and revenue below, but keep in mind there's always more to the story.

We note that hedge funds don't have a meaningful investment in Hankook Tire & Technology. Hankook & Company Co., Ltd. is currently the company's largest shareholder with 31% of shares outstanding. Hyun-Bum Cho is the second largest shareholder owning 7.9% of common stock, and National Pension Service holds about 7.8% of the company stock.

Our research also brought to light the fact that roughly 53% of the company is controlled by the top 4 shareholders suggesting that these owners wield significant influence on the business.

While studying institutional ownership for a company can add value to your research, it is also a good practice to research analyst recommendations to get a deeper understand of a stock's expected performance. There are plenty of analysts covering the stock, so it might be worth seeing what they are forecasting, too.

Insider Ownership Of Hankook Tire & Technology

The definition of company insiders can be subjective and does vary between jurisdictions. Our data reflects individual insiders, capturing board members at the very least. The company management answer to the board and the latter should represent the interests of shareholders. Notably, sometimes top-level managers are on the board themselves.

Most consider insider ownership a positive because it can indicate the board is well aligned with other shareholders. However, on some occasions too much power is concentrated within this group.

Our most recent data indicates that insiders own a reasonable proportion of Hankook Tire & Technology Co., Ltd.. Insiders own ₩1.0t worth of shares in the ₩8.2t company. That's quite meaningful. It is good to see this level of investment. You can check here to see if those insiders have been buying recently.

General Public Ownership

The general public, who are usually individual investors, hold a 26% stake in Hankook Tire & Technology. While this group can't necessarily call the shots, it can certainly have a real influence on how the company is run.

Public Company Ownership

Public companies currently own 31% of Hankook Tire & Technology stock. We can't be certain but it is quite possible this is a strategic stake. The businesses may be similar, or work together.

Next Steps:

I find it very interesting to look at who exactly owns a company. But to truly gain insight, we need to consider other information, too. Case in point: We've spotted 2 warning signs for Hankook Tire & Technology you should be aware of.

Ultimately the future is most important. You can access this free report on analyst forecasts for the company.

NB: Figures in this article are calculated using data from the last twelve months, which refer to the 12-month period ending on the last date of the month the financial statement is dated. This may not be consistent with full year annual report figures.

data.title: The Returns At Hyundai Autoever (KRX:307950) Aren't Growing
data.description:
Finding a business that has the potential to grow substantially is not easy, but it is possible if we look at a few key financial metrics. One common approach is to try and find a company with returns on capital employed (ROCE) that are increasing, in conjunction with a growing amount of capital employed. Ultimately, this demonstrates that it's a business that is reinvesting profits at increasing rates of return. With that in mind, the ROCE of Hyundai Autoever (KRX:307950) looks decent, right now, so lets see what the trend of returns can tell us.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

Just to clarify if you're unsure, ROCE is a metric for evaluating how much pre-tax income (in percentage terms) a company earns on the capital invested in its business. Analysts use this formula to calculate it for Hyundai Autoever:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.12 = ₩251b ÷ (₩3.3t - ₩1.1t) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, Hyundai Autoever has an ROCE of 12%. In absolute terms, that's a satisfactory return, but compared to the IT industry average of 9.0% it's much better.

View our latest analysis for Hyundai Autoever

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Hyundai Autoever compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free analyst report for Hyundai Autoever .

The Trend Of ROCE

While the current returns on capital are decent, they haven't changed much. The company has employed 239% more capital in the last five years, and the returns on that capital have remained stable at 12%. Since 12% is a moderate ROCE though, it's good to see a business can continue to reinvest at these decent rates of return. Over long periods of time, returns like these might not be too exciting, but with consistency they can pay off in terms of share price returns.

What We Can Learn From Hyundai Autoever's ROCE

To sum it up, Hyundai Autoever has simply been reinvesting capital steadily, at those decent rates of return. And the stock has done incredibly well with a 280% return over the last five years, so long term investors are no doubt ecstatic with that result. So even though the stock might be more "expensive" than it was before, we think the strong fundamentals warrant this stock for further research.

One more thing, we've spotted 1 warning sign facing Hyundai Autoever that you might find interesting.

While Hyundai Autoever isn't earning the highest return, check out this free list of companies that are earning high returns on equity with solid balance sheets.

data.title: Palantir Deepens Commercial Roots With HD Hyundai And Sovereign AI Push
data.description:
• Palantir Technologies (NasdaqGS:PLTR) has entered a large, multi-year commercial partnership with South Korea's HD Hyundai, covering shipbuilding, construction, robotics, and electric systems.
  • The agreement places Palantir's AI platforms at the center of HD Hyundai's group-wide digital transformation plans.
  • Separately, Palantir is working on sovereign-grade AI data centers across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa for commercial and government use.

Palantir, known for its data and AI software platforms, has been expanding from its government roots into broader commercial use. The HD Hyundai partnership and sovereign AI work in EMEA show how its tools are being applied to heavy industry and critical infrastructure rather than just software or internet companies.

For you as an investor, these announcements highlight where Palantir is trying to build long-term, embedded roles inside large organizations and public systems. The scale and duration of these partnerships could influence how you think about the company’s international footprint and mix between commercial and government related revenue over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Palantir Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Palantir Technologies.

How Palantir Technologies stacks up against its biggest competitors

For Palantir, the HD Hyundai deal and the Sovereign AI data center work in EMEA both point to the same thing: its software sitting deeper inside critical operational systems rather than at the edge of workflows. If these projects continue as described, they could increase the portion of revenue tied to long-duration, infrastructure style deployments across heavy industry and regulated sectors.

Advertisement

Palantir Technologies Narrative, Commercial Pivot And AI Story

These agreements sit alongside an existing narrative that Palantir is pushing harder into commercial use cases and AI heavy workloads, including its Artificial Intelligence Platform. For investors who follow the story of Palantir as a company that wants to be embedded in mission critical projects, this news fits with that theme across both industrial customers like HD Hyundai and sovereign focused AI infrastructure in EMEA.

Risks And Rewards To Keep In Mind

  • 🎁 Large, multi year deployments with HD Hyundai and Sovereign AI could support more recurring, infrastructure like software usage across energy, shipbuilding, construction, and government related work.
  • 🎁 Working with partners such as Accenture, Dell, and NVIDIA on sovereign grade AI centers may help Palantir position its platforms as part of broader AI stacks that are already in demand, such as the 60% of European organizations that plan to increase sovereign AI investment.
  • ⚠️ Execution risk is meaningful. Integrating Foundry and AIP across an entire industrial group or into new regional data center projects can be complex and may take longer or cost more than expected.
  • ⚠️ Concentration around high profile AI projects and sovereign infrastructure ties Palantir to policy decisions, regulatory scrutiny, and changing procurement priorities in both commercial and public sectors.

What To Watch Next

From here, you might watch for concrete updates on adoption across HD Hyundai subsidiaries, milestones on the EMEA data center rollout, and how these contracts show up in Palantir’s commercial and international revenue mix over time. You can also stay close to how other investors interpret this story by following the latest community views at this dedicated narratives hub.

data.title: Why Chalice Mining (ASX:CHN) Is Up 10.7% After Gonneville Funding Plan And Insider Share Sale
data.description:
• Recent reports highlight Chalice Mining’s Gonneville palladium-nickel-copper project in Western Australia, which the company says could materially influence global palladium supply and is currently planned for a two-stage development with A$820 million in initial capital expenditure, largely funded by debt, following anticipated regulatory approvals in early 2028.
  • At the same time, Managing Director Alexander Carl Dorsch’s sale of 1.5 million shares at A$2.30 each has sharpened market focus on both the project’s long-term importance and insider alignment with shareholders.
  • We’ll now examine how the Gonneville project’s potential to reshape palladium supply influences Chalice Mining’s investment narrative.

We've found 14 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Chalice Mining's Investment Narrative?

To own Chalice Mining today, you really have to believe the Gonneville project will ultimately justify years of losses and dilution risk, given the company’s minimal current revenue and ongoing A$24.21 million annual loss. The latest Gonneville study positions the asset as a potentially meaningful new source of palladium, nickel and copper, with an A$820 million first-stage spend largely debt funded, so the near term story still revolves around securing approvals, project financing and cost control rather than earnings. Recent share price strength suggests that message is getting some traction, but Alexander Dorsch’s sale of 1.5 million shares at A$2.30 has sharpened attention on insider alignment at a time when investors already face funding, execution and commodity price risks. For now, this sale looks more sentiment related than thesis changing, yet it feeds into the question of who ultimately shoulders the project risk: lenders, new equity holders or both.

However, one particular funding risk here is something investors really should understand. Chalice Mining's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Six fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly A$0.56 to A$5.55, reflecting very different views on Gonneville’s future. Set against Gonneville’s A$820 million funding need and Chalice’s continuing losses, these contrasting opinions underline why understanding both project risk and funding options matters before forming your own view.

data.title: Could Lucerastat’s Renal Signal Reframe Idorsia (SWX:IDIA)’s Rare-Disease Strategy And Risk Profile?
data.description:
• Idorsia Ltd. recently reported in Nature Communications the Phase 3 MODIFY and open-label extension results for lucerastat, an oral substrate reduction therapy in adults with Fabry disease, which did not meet its primary neuropathic pain endpoint but showed sustained biomarker reductions and encouraging renal outcomes.
  • The data, including a slower rate of eGFR decline and stabilization of cardiac measures in patients with impaired kidney function, have guided the design of a new Phase 3 program being discussed with the US FDA.
  • We’ll now examine how lucerastat’s renal function signal and potential disease-modifying profile could influence Idorsia’s broader investment narrative.

Rare earth metals are the new gold rush. Find out which 32 stocks are leading the charge.

Advertisement

What Is Idorsia's Investment Narrative?

For Idorsia, being a shareholder hinges on believing the company can convert its late-stage pipeline and new commercial products into a sustainable business despite ongoing losses and recent dilution. In the near term, the main catalysts still sit with commercialization of aprocitentan (TRYVIO/JERAYGO) in resistant hypertension and execution on QUVIVIQ, while the CCR6 psoriasis program quietly adds optionality. The newly published lucerastat data slightly shift the story: Fabry disease moves back into the conversation as a potential medium-term value driver, but only if the renal signal can be translated into a registrational Phase 3 acceptable to regulators. Until that is clearer, the MODIFY/OLE results primarily strengthen Idorsia’s scientific credibility rather than its revenue outlook, leaving balance sheet pressure and the path to profitability as the key near-term risks.

However, Idorsia’s negative equity position remains a crucial issue investors should not overlook. According our valuation report, there's an indication that Idorsia's share price might be on the expensive side.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Three Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates for Idorsia cluster between CHF4.00 and CHF5.86, reflecting very different expectations. When you set that against the company’s continuing losses and dependence on future trial and launch execution, it underlines why examining multiple viewpoints on Idorsia’s prospects really matters.

data.title: Assessing Hasbro (HAS) Valuation After New Board Appointments Of Doug Bowser And Carla Vernón
data.description:
Hasbro (HAS) has drawn fresh attention after adding Doug Bowser, former President and COO of Nintendo of America, and Carla Vernón, CEO of The Honest Company, to its Board of Directors.

See our latest analysis for Hasbro.

The board appointments arrive at a time when short term momentum has been picking up, with a 30-day share price return of 7.41% and a 90-day gain of 13.56%. At the same time, the 1-year total shareholder return of 60.60% and 3-year total shareholder return of 73.50% point to a much stronger longer term outcome than the recent 6.88% year to date share price return might suggest.

If you are looking beyond toys and games, this could be a useful moment to broaden your watchlist and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Hasbro trading at $88.68, sitting close to a consensus price target of $93.54 but at a reported 40.20% discount to intrinsic value, you have to ask: is there still upside here, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 5.2% Undervalued

Hasbro's most followed narrative pegs fair value at $93.54, only slightly above the current $88.68 share price, which keeps expectations grounded but still interesting.

Rapidly growing cross platform digital gaming and licensing revenue, exemplified by Wizards of the Coast (notably Magic: The Gathering's 23%+ YoY growth and MONOPOLY GO!), is expanding Hasbro's addressable market and recurring high margin earnings streams, positioning the company to capitalize on the global rise of digital entertainment, which should drive outsized revenue and operating profit growth.

Read the complete narrative.

However, the story can change quickly if demand for core franchises softens or if tariffs and supply chain costs put pressure on the margin assumptions behind that fair value.

Another View: Multiples Paint A Tougher Picture

The fair value narrative leans on future cash flows and suggests Hasbro is 40.2% below intrinsic value, yet the market is not giving the shares away based on today’s sales. On a P/S of 2.9x, Hasbro trades above the US Leisure industry at 1x and above peer averages at 1.3x, and also above its own fair ratio of 2.2x.

That gap implies investors are already paying a premium for $4.4b of revenue while the company is still loss making. As a result, anyone attracted to the DCF upside needs to weigh how much rerating room is truly left if sentiment around earnings or margins cools.

data.title: Assessing Australian Foundation Investment’s Valuation After Half Year Profit Decline And New Buyback Program
data.description:
Advertisement

Why AFIC’s latest earnings, dividend and buyback matter for investors

Australian Foundation Investment (ASX:AFI) has just reported its half year result with profit softer than a year earlier, while at the same time confirming a fully franked interim dividend and a special payout to shareholders.

The Board has also approved a buyback that covers up to 8.93% of issued capital. This gives investors several moving parts to weigh up around income, capital management and AFIC’s long term investment approach.

See our latest analysis for Australian Foundation Investment.

AFIC’s recent dividend increase and the newly authorised buyback have come as the share price has softened over the past quarter, with a 90 day share price return of 2.98% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of 1.76% decline. This suggests momentum has been fading even as the Board leans into capital management to support investor confidence.

If AFIC’s mix of dividends and buybacks has you thinking about where else capital might work hard, it could be a good time to broaden your view with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With profit and portfolio returns softer, yet a fully franked dividend, special payout and sizeable buyback all on the table, is AFIC quietly offering value today or is the market already pricing in its future growth?

Preferred P/E of 32.3x, is it justified?

AFIC currently trades on a P/E of 32.3x, which lines up with a share price of A$7.16 and raises questions about how the market is treating its earnings stream.

The P/E ratio compares the share price to earnings per share, so a higher P/E often means investors are willing to pay more today for each dollar of profit. For an investment company like AFIC, that can reflect how investors view the quality and stability of its earnings, as well as expectations about the long term portfolio performance that drives those earnings.

Against its closest peer group, AFIC screens as good value compared with similar companies, where the peer average multiple sits at 75.9x earnings. However, compared with the broader Australian Capital Markets industry on 21.2x, AFIC looks more expensive, which suggests investors may be treating it differently to the wider sector. That tension between being cheaper than peers yet richer than the industry average is what many investors will focus on when deciding whether this P/E feels comfortable.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

Result: Price-to-earnings of 32.3x (ABOUT RIGHT)

However, the recent 90-day share price return decline of 2.98% and 1-year total return decline of 1.76% could signal weaker sentiment that challenges this valuation.

Find out about the key risks to this Australian Foundation Investment narrative.

Another angle from our DCF model

While the 32.3x P/E suggests AFIC is roughly in line with what the market is currently paying for its earnings, our DCF model points in a very different direction. On this view, the shares at A$7.16 sit well above an estimated future cash flow value of A$0.44, which implies a wide gap between price and intrinsic value. For you as an investor, that kind of disconnect can either signal caution or a belief that the model is missing key parts of AFIC’s story. Which side of that fence do you sit on?

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Australian Foundation Investment for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 874 undervalued stocks based on their cash flows. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

data.title: A Look At Texas Pacific Land (TPL) Valuation After New Partnership And Insider Buying
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Texas Pacific Land is back on investors’ radar

Texas Pacific Land (TPL) has drawn fresh attention after agreeing to partner with Bolt Data & Energy on large-scale data center campuses and committing a US$50 million investment tied to equity and water supply rights.

See our latest analysis for Texas Pacific Land.

The recent partnership announcement and insider buying interest have come alongside a 20.66% 1 month share price return and a 16.43% year to date share price return, set against a 23.00% decline in 1 year total shareholder return and a 299.01% 5 year total shareholder return. Taken together, these figures suggest short term momentum on top of a longer term record of strong compounding for investors who stayed the course.

If this kind of infrastructure themed story has caught your attention, it could be a good moment to widen your search and check out fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With Texas Pacific Land trading at US$346.94 alongside mixed signals from analyst targets, intrinsic estimates and strong insider buying, the real question is whether you are looking at an overlooked compounder or a stock where future growth is already priced in.

Most Popular Narrative: 23.5% Overvalued

Against the latest close of $346.94, the most followed narrative puts Texas Pacific Land’s fair value at $280.83, creating a clear valuation gap to unpack.

The analysts have a consensus price target of $921.93 for Texas Pacific Land based on their expectations of its future earnings growth, profit margins and other risk factors.

In order for you to agree with the analyst's consensus, you'd need to believe that by 2028, revenues will be $895.3 million, earnings will come to $610.3 million, and it would be trading on a PE ratio of 41.9x, assuming you use a discount rate of 6.4%.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what sits behind that lower fair value, despite confident growth and margin assumptions and a premium earnings multiple years out, all run through a 6.956% discount rate?

However, sustained oil and gas royalty production and expanding water services, supported by high margins and a net cash position, could challenge the idea that TPL is overvalued.

data.title: How Sysco’s Expense Discipline and Volume Momentum At Sysco (SYY) Has Changed Its Investment Story
data.description:
• Sysco Corporation is approaching its fiscal 2026 second-quarter earnings release, with analysts expecting year-over-year gains in both earnings per share and revenue, supported by stronger U.S. Foodservice volumes and contributions from its International segment despite the prior Mexico joint venture divestiture.
  • An interesting element of this setup is management’s emphasis on structural improvements, such as disciplined expense management and volume momentum, as key drivers of anticipated earnings growth rather than relying solely on headline sales expansion.
  • Next, we’ll examine how expectations for disciplined expense management could influence Sysco’s broader investment narrative following its recent share price moves.

Explore 23 top quantum computing companies leading the revolution in next-gen technology and shaping the future with breakthroughs in quantum algorithms, superconducting qubits, and cutting-edge research.

Advertisement

What Is Sysco's Investment Narrative?

To own Sysco, you need to be comfortable with a mature, highly leveraged distributor that aims to turn modest revenue growth into steadier profit gains through execution rather than big market swings. The latest earnings expectations, calling for mid single digit growth in revenue and earnings per share, mostly reinforce that story instead of changing it. Near term, the key catalyst remains whether Sysco can show that disciplined expense management and stronger U.S. Foodservice volumes translate into cleaner margin trends after a period of softer profit growth and margin compression. The International segment’s contribution, even after the Mexico joint venture sale, matters more as a proof point than a growth engine. The biggest risk that has not gone away is the combination of thin margins and meaningful debt.

However, Sysco’s high leverage and thin margins remain a key issue investors should understand. Despite retreating, Sysco's shares might still be trading above their fair value and there could be some more downside. Discover how much.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Two fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly US$87 to US$161 per share, reflecting very different expectations. Set against management’s focus on cost discipline and volume mix, this wide gap underlines how much opinions diverge on whether Sysco’s execution can offset its debt load and low margins over time.

data.title: How Investors May Respond To Mirum Pharmaceuticals (MIRM) 2026 Revenue Guidance Centered On Existing Drugs And Pipeline
data.description:
• In January 2026, Mirum Pharmaceuticals issued earnings guidance for 2026, projecting total product revenue of US$630 million to US$650 million.
  • This outlook highlights the company’s confidence in its existing products and late-stage clinical pipeline as key revenue contributors.
  • Next, we’ll examine how Mirum’s revenue guidance for 2026 shapes the company’s investment narrative and longer-term commercial profile.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 109 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10b in market cap - there's still time to get in early.

Advertisement

What Is Mirum Pharmaceuticals' Investment Narrative?

To own Mirum today, you really have to believe that its rare‑disease franchise, led by LIVMARLI and supported by a broader cholestatic and genetic disorder pipeline, can translate clinical traction into durable, high‑margin product sales. The new 2026 revenue guidance of US$630 million to US$650 million reinforces that story in the near term, building on the recent raise to 2025 guidance and the strong share price run over the past year. In the short term, the key catalysts still sit with pivotal and Phase 2b readouts across cholestatic indications and integration of the Bluejay acquisition, but this guidance slightly de‑risks the commercial side by anchoring expectations around existing products. At the same time, it sharpens the biggest risks: clinical setbacks, execution missteps on new indications and the need to justify a rich revenue multiple after such a large multi‑year share price move.

However, one risk around Mirum’s reliance on upcoming trial results is easy to overlook. Mirum Pharmaceuticals' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued. Find out how large the opportunity might be.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s five fair value estimates span roughly US$34 to over US$374, showing very different views on Mirum’s potential. Set against management’s confident 2026 revenue guidance and dependence on late stage trial outcomes, that spread underlines how differently investors can weigh near term clinical and commercial risks.
